zones.name: Afghanistan
zones.lore: High-risk operating environment with severe security and mobility constraints.

quests.label: Severe security constraints
quests.detail: Entry, movement, and long-stay activity can be highly restricted.

zones.name: Albania
zones.lore: Lower-cost Balkan route with rising tourism and services sectors.

quests.label: Residence and work permit process
quests.detail: Long-stay work typically requires employer-backed permits.

zones.name: Algeria
zones.lore: Large Maghreb economy with strong hydrocarbon base and growing regional logistics relevance.

quests.label: Work permit and residence card
quests.detail: Long-stay employment usually requires sponsor-backed approvals.

zones.name: Angola
zones.lore: Oil and resource-linked economy with growing logistics and urban service sectors.

quests.label: Work visa and residence permit
quests.detail: Employer-backed status and registration are required for long-term work.

zones.name: Antarctica
zones.lore: Antarctica is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Argentina
zones.lore: Large South American economy with strong education base, but macro volatility and inflation add planning difficulty.

quests.label: Temporary residency pathway
quests.detail: Work, study, or investment pathways available with renewals.

zones.name: Armenia
zones.lore: Small Caucasus economy with strong tech talent and diaspora-linked investment.

quests.label: Residence registration pathway
quests.detail: Work/business-based legal stay routes are comparatively accessible.

zones.name: Australia
zones.lore: Sun-scorched and friendly, this island continent offers one of the world's most livable environments. The points-based system rewards skilled adventurers generously.

quests.label: Australian Skilled Visa
quests.detail: Skilled Independent (189), State Nominated (190), or employer-sponsored (482).

quests.label: Skills Assessment
quests.detail: Occupation must appear on skills shortage list; formal assessment required.

zones.name: Austria
zones.lore: High-quality Alpine economy with strong engineering and services sectors.

quests.label: Red-White-Red Card
quests.detail: Points-based route for skilled workers and shortage occupations.

zones.name: Azerbaijan
zones.lore: Caspian economy with strong energy base and expanding transport corridors.

quests.label: Temporary residence/work permit
quests.detail: Work and stay generally require employer or project backing.

zones.name: Bangladesh
zones.lore: Dense manufacturing and export economy with rapidly growing digital and services sectors.

quests.label: Work permit and visa compliance
quests.detail: Foreign employment generally requires sponsoring employer and approvals.

zones.name: Belarus
zones.lore: Sanctions-impacted environment with elevated geopolitical and compliance risk.

quests.label: High compliance and sanctions complexity
quests.detail: Permits and financial/legal operations can be significantly constrained.

zones.name: Belgium
zones.lore: EU administrative core with multilingual labor markets and dense logistics corridors.

quests.label: Single Permit
quests.detail: Combined work and residence permit for most non-EU employees.

zones.name: Belize
zones.lore: English-speaking Central American economy with tourism and expat-friendly pathways.

quests.label: Residence permit pathways
quests.detail: Employment and retirement routes exist with local documentation.

zones.name: Benin
zones.lore: West African coastal economy with trade, agriculture, and logistics opportunities.

quests.label: Work/residence permit pathway
quests.detail: Employer or business-backed legal stay documents are typically required.

zones.name: Bhutan
zones.lore: Small Himalayan state with tightly managed tourism and conservative migration policy.

quests.label: Restricted long-stay pathways
quests.detail: Employment and residency routes are selective and policy-controlled.

zones.name: Bolivia
zones.lore: Andean interior economy with low costs and growing urban service nodes.

quests.label: Temporary residence categories
quests.detail: Work and business pathways available with local documentation.

zones.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
zones.lore: Western Balkan economy with moderate costs and growing services output.

quests.label: Work permit and temporary residence
quests.detail: Employer-sponsored permit is common for non-local workers.

zones.name: Botswana
zones.lore: Governance-stable Southern African state with mining wealth and steady public institutions.

quests.label: Work and residence permit track
quests.detail: Employer sponsorship and professional need are key factors.

zones.name: Brazil
zones.lore: Vast, chaotic, and full of opportunity. Safety stats vary enormously between sub-zones. The most biodiverse zone on the map.

quests.label: Brazil Residence Visa
quests.detail: Permanent visa via investment, retirement, or family. Digital nomads enter visa-free (90 days).

zones.name: Brunei
zones.lore: High-income microstate with hydrocarbon-backed economy and strict labor governance.

quests.label: Employment pass and sponsor approvals
quests.detail: Most foreign workers require employer-sponsored permit routes.

zones.name: Bulgaria
zones.lore: Cost-efficient EU location with growing outsourcing and technology sectors.

quests.label: Long-stay visa D + residence
quests.detail: Employer sponsorship or specific residence purpose required.

zones.name: Burkina Faso
zones.lore: Sahelian economy with agriculture-led fundamentals and rising urban trade activity.

quests.label: Work permit and local registration
quests.detail: Employer-backed paperwork and legal registration required for long-term work.

zones.name: Burundi
zones.lore: Densely populated Great Lakes state with agriculture and trade-centered local economies.

quests.label: Residence/work authorization process
quests.detail: Long-stay status generally depends on documented work or business purpose.

zones.name: Cambodia
zones.lore: Lower-cost Southeast Asian economy with expanding manufacturing and tourism sectors.

quests.label: Business visa + work permit
quests.detail: Employment requires annual permit compliance and documentation.

zones.name: Cameroon
zones.lore: Resource-diverse economy linking West and Central Africa through transport and trade corridors.

quests.label: Work authorization and residency
quests.detail: Employer-backed permits and local documentation required.

zones.name: Canada
zones.lore: A vast and welcoming expanse with more immigration pathways than almost any zone on the map. High quality of life, though the northern winters are legendary in their severity.

quests.label: Permanent Residency or Work Permit
quests.detail: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker), PNP, or employer-specific work permits.

quests.label: Language Test (IELTS/CELPIP)
quests.detail: CLB 7 minimum for Express Entry. French equivalent accepted.

zones.name: Central African Republic
zones.lore: Fragile governance context with severe security and logistics constraints.

quests.label: Severe security and access constraints
quests.detail: Operational movement and long-stay activity can be heavily restricted.

zones.name: Chad
zones.lore: Landlocked Sahel state with high insecurity and limited service infrastructure.

quests.label: Severe security and access constraints
quests.detail: Work and movement often require elevated controls and contingency planning.

zones.name: Chile
zones.lore: Andean-Pacific economy with stable institutions and strong mining/energy sectors.

quests.label: Temporary residence categories
quests.detail: Routes for work contracts, professional services, and entrepreneurship.

zones.name: Colombia
zones.lore: Colombia's transformation is one of the great redemption arcs in the zone atlas. Medellín is now a startup beacon; Cartagena a colonial gem. Safety varies sharply by district.

quests.label: Colombia Digital Nomad or Migrant Visa
quests.detail: Digital Nomad Visa (up to 2 years) or Migrant Visa (M) for longer stays.

zones.name: Costa Rica
zones.lore: Pura vida is not a marketing slogan — it's the zone's actual operating system. Stable democracy, no standing army, and biodiversity that rivals any zone on earth.

quests.label: Rentista or Pensionado Visa
quests.detail: Rentista requires $2,500/month passive income. Digital Nomad Visa (1 year) also available.

zones.name: Croatia
zones.lore: Adriatic destination with growing remote-work and tourism-oriented economy.

quests.label: Temporary stay pathways
quests.detail: Includes work-based permits and digital nomad options.

zones.name: Cuba
zones.lore: Large Caribbean island economy with strong healthcare and tourism systems under unique regulatory structure.

quests.label: Entry and long-stay authorization
quests.detail: Residency and work routes are tightly regulated and context-specific.

zones.name: Cyprus
zones.lore: EU island economy with strong professional services and shipping sectors.

quests.label: Residence/work permit framework
quests.detail: Employment and business pathways available with registration requirements.

zones.name: Czech Republic
zones.lore: Prague remains one of the most beautiful capitals in the known world. Increasingly tech-forward and affordable by Western European standards — a hidden gem tier zone.

quests.label: Czech Employee Card or Blue Card
quests.detail: Employee Card combines work permit and residence permit. EU Blue Card for high earners.

zones.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
zones.lore: Resource-rich but highly challenging operational environment with major regional variability.

quests.label: High-risk entry and mobility constraints
quests.detail: Security and compliance conditions vary sharply by province.

zones.name: Denmark
zones.lore: Highly stable welfare state with strong work-life balance and high salary/high tax equilibrium.

quests.label: Positive List or Pay Limit Scheme
quests.detail: Work permit routes tied to shortage occupations and salary floors.

zones.name: Djibouti
zones.lore: Strategic Red Sea chokepoint economy centered on ports, bases, and logistics.

quests.label: Work visa and residence permit
quests.detail: Long-term employment is typically sponsor-linked.

zones.name: Dominican Republic
zones.lore: Caribbean services and tourism economy with growing nearshore business operations.

quests.label: Temporary residence pathways
quests.detail: Work, investor, and pensioner routes available with documentation.

zones.name: East Timor
zones.lore: East Timor is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Ecuador
zones.lore: Dollarized Andean economy with manageable costs and diverse climate/economic zones.

quests.label: Temporary residency categories
quests.detail: Work, professional, or investment visas are common routes.

zones.name: Egypt
zones.lore: Large regional economy anchored by Cairo and Alexandria, with logistics and tourism scale.

quests.label: Work permit and residency process
quests.detail: Sponsorship and permit approvals vary by role and employer.

zones.name: El Salvador
zones.lore: Compact economy with growing services and digital-finance ambitions.

quests.label: Temporary residence + work permit
quests.detail: Employer-backed residence categories are typical for long-term work.

zones.name: Equatorial Guinea
zones.lore: Small oil-linked economy with concentrated urban-industrial opportunities.

quests.label: Work permit and residency authorization
quests.detail: Sponsor-backed permits and compliance are needed for legal employment.

zones.name: Eritrea
zones.lore: Highly restricted policy environment with limited external labor mobility pathways.

quests.label: Highly restricted entry framework
quests.detail: Long-stay and work authorizations are limited and policy-constrained.

zones.name: Estonia
zones.lore: Highly digital Baltic state with startup-friendly governance and lean administration.

quests.label: Digital Nomad / employment permit
quests.detail: Remote work and startup routes are relatively streamlined.

zones.name: Eswatini
zones.lore: Eswatini is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Ethiopia
zones.lore: Large and fast-growing East African economy with major infrastructure expansion.

quests.label: Work permit and residence process
quests.detail: Employment-based status requires sponsor filings and registrations.

zones.name: Falkland Islands
zones.lore: Falkland Islands is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Fiji
zones.lore: Fiji is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Finland
zones.lore: High-trust Nordic state with strong tech and education outcomes and high living costs.

quests.label: Residence permit (work/study)
quests.detail: Job contract or admission plus income support proof.

zones.name: France
zones.lore: Home of art, gastronomy, and the most complex administrative system in Europe. The Talent Passport is France's attempt to simplify access for skilled adventurers.

quests.label: Talent Passport or Work Permit
quests.detail: Talent Passport covers 10 categories (researchers, founders, artists).

zones.name: French Southern and Antarctic Lands
zones.lore: French Southern and Antarctic Lands is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Gabon
zones.lore: Small high-income African economy with energy and forestry strengths.

quests.label: Work visa and residence process
quests.detail: Employment sponsorship and permit validation are required.

zones.name: Georgia
zones.lore: Caucasus transit hub with liberal entry options and rising startup/tourism profile.

quests.label: Residence permit pathways
quests.detail: Business, work, and property-linked options are commonly used.

zones.name: Germany
zones.lore: An engineering stronghold of exceptional efficiency. The Opportunity Visa now opens a side gate for skilled adventurers — but mastering the local tongue unlocks the true endgame.

quests.label: German Work Visa or EU Blue Card
quests.detail: Skilled worker visa, EU Blue Card, or Chancenkarte (job seeker visa) required.

quests.label: Qualification Recognition
quests.detail: Foreign degrees must be officially recognized via the anabin database.

quests.label: German B1 Certificate
quests.detail: Required for citizenship; strongly preferred for most employers.

zones.name: Ghana
zones.lore: The most politically stable zone in West Africa. Ghana's Year of Return program opened its gates to the global diaspora, and the momentum has not stopped.

quests.label: Ghana Residence or Right of Abode
quests.detail: Right of Abode for African diaspora; work permit for skilled professionals.

zones.name: Greece
zones.lore: Mediterranean economy balancing tourism, shipping, and growing digital services hubs.

quests.label: Work/residence permit pathway
quests.detail: Employer-backed permits and long-stay routes vary by profession.

zones.name: Greenland
zones.lore: Greenland is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Guatemala
zones.lore: Central American economy with expanding manufacturing and services opportunities.

quests.label: Residency and work authorization
quests.detail: Long-stay work routes generally require sponsor-backed filings.

zones.name: Guinea
zones.lore: Guinea is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Guinea-Bissau
zones.lore: Guinea-Bissau is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Guyana
zones.lore: Guyana is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Haiti
zones.lore: Haiti is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Honduras
zones.lore: Central American market with expanding manufacturing corridors and service-sector growth.

quests.label: Residence and work authorization route
quests.detail: Employer-backed documents and long-stay registration required.

zones.name: Hungary
zones.lore: Central European growth economy with strong manufacturing and shared-services sectors.

quests.label: Single Permit for work and stay
quests.detail: Employment contract and permit category determine entry route.

zones.name: Iceland
zones.lore: High-income North Atlantic economy with excellent governance and high living costs.

quests.label: Residence and work permit
quests.detail: Non-EEA routes generally need employer sponsorship and role eligibility.

zones.name: India
zones.lore: Massive and diverse growth economy with globally competitive tech and services hubs.

quests.label: Visa class alignment
quests.detail: Employment, business, and OCI pathways vary by origin and role.

zones.name: Indonesia
zones.lore: An archipelago of 17,000 islands. Bali's Second Home Visa has made it a legendary nomad destination. Cost-of-living buffs are exceptional.

quests.label: Second Home Visa or KITAS
quests.detail: Second Home Visa (5-10 years, ~$130,000 investment) or KITAS work permit.

zones.name: Iran
zones.lore: Large regional economy with strong technical talent under complex sanctions and regulatory constraints.

quests.label: Sanctions-constrained operating environment
quests.detail: Entry, payments, and legal compliance can be materially restricted.

zones.name: Iraq
zones.lore: Energy-rich country with uneven security and infrastructure conditions across regions.

quests.label: Security-sensitive entry process
quests.detail: Permit and entry requirements vary significantly by purpose and region.

zones.name: Ireland
zones.lore: English-speaking EU hub with outsized presence of global tech and pharma.

quests.label: Critical Skills Employment Permit
quests.detail: Fast-track route for high-demand occupations and salary thresholds.

zones.name: Israel
zones.lore: Innovation-heavy economy with deep startup density and high cost in central metro zones.

quests.label: B/1 work visa pathway
quests.detail: Employment requires sponsor-backed permit and status compliance.

zones.name: Italy
zones.lore: High cultural quality-of-life and industrial clusters, with regionally varied economies and administrative overhead.

quests.label: Work visa + Permesso di Soggiorno
quests.detail: Permit and residence registration required post-arrival.

zones.name: Cote d'Ivoire
zones.lore: Francophone West African growth hub with strong trade and agribusiness expansion.

quests.label: Work and residence card process
quests.detail: Entry and long-stay status typically tied to sponsor/employer.

zones.name: Jamaica
zones.lore: Tourism and services-led Caribbean economy with strong cultural export influence.

quests.label: Work permit and residence permission
quests.detail: Long-term work generally requires employer-backed permits.

zones.name: Japan
zones.lore: An ancient fortress rebuilt as a gleaming tech nexus. Breathtakingly safe and orderly, but the language wall is one of the steepest in the known world.

quests.label: Japan Work Visa
quests.detail: Work visa tied to profession (Engineer/Specialist, Skilled Labor, HSP points-based).

quests.label: JLPT N3 or Higher
quests.detail: Not legally required, but practically essential for daily life and most workplaces.

zones.name: Jordan
zones.lore: Stable Levantine hub with strengths in logistics, healthcare, and education services.

quests.label: Work permit and residence card
quests.detail: Employer sponsorship and permit approvals are required for most foreign workers.

zones.name: Kazakhstan
zones.lore: Resource-rich Eurasian state with rising logistics corridors and energy opportunities.

quests.label: Work permit and temporary residence
quests.detail: Foreign labor quotas and employer sponsorship apply.

zones.name: Kenya
zones.lore: East African economic anchor with strong fintech, logistics, and regional trade connectivity.

quests.label: Work permit and residence process
quests.detail: Employment permit categories and local registration required.

zones.name: Kuwait
zones.lore: Oil-funded Gulf economy with high-income labor market and sponsor-based migration pathways.

quests.label: Sponsor-led work permit process
quests.detail: Work authorization is employer-linked for most foreign workers.

zones.name: Kyrgyzstan
zones.lore: Mountainous Central Asian economy with trade, agriculture, and services growth corridors.

quests.label: Residence registration and permit process
quests.detail: Long-term legal stay depends on status type and local registration.

zones.name: Laos
zones.lore: Landlocked Mekong economy with gradual infrastructure growth and low baseline costs.

quests.label: Business/work visa and permit process
quests.detail: Employer or enterprise sponsorship is common for legal long-term work.

zones.name: Latvia
zones.lore: Baltic service economy with strategic logistics position and moderate living costs.

quests.label: Work visa and residence permit
quests.detail: Employment-driven permits are primary route for long-term stay.

zones.name: Lebanon
zones.lore: Historic Levant hub under persistent economic stress and institutional uncertainty.

quests.label: Volatile operating environment
quests.detail: Entry and long-term settlement require heightened due diligence.

zones.lore: Lesotho is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.lore: Liberia is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Lithuania
zones.lore: Digitally advanced Baltic economy with competitive fintech and service ecosystems.

quests.label: National visa D / temporary residence
quests.detail: Work and startup pathways exist with employer or business basis.

zones.name: Luxembourg
zones.lore: Luxembourg is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Madagascar
zones.lore: Large island ecosystem with agriculture, tourism, and resource opportunities under infrastructure constraints.

quests.label: Long-stay visa + work permit
quests.detail: Business and employment status need permit and registration follow-through.

zones.name: Malawi
zones.lore: Malawi is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Malaysia
zones.lore: Multiethnic, multilingual, and magnificently affordable. Malaysia's DE Rantau nomad visa is one of Southeast Asia's most welcoming. KL punches well above its weight.

quests.label: DE Rantau Nomad Visa or MM2H
quests.detail: DE Rantau for digital workers (12 months); MM2H for long-term residents (10 years).

zones.name: Mali
zones.lore: Sahelian state with high operational risk and regionally variable security conditions.

quests.label: High-risk access and movement constraints
quests.detail: Travel, work, and residence can be heavily constrained by local conditions.

zones.name: Mauritania
zones.lore: Sparsely populated Saharan economy with mining and port-linked trade activity.

quests.label: Work permit and residence card
quests.detail: Foreign employment requires sponsorship and administrative registration.

zones.name: Mexico
zones.lore: A sprawling, vibrant zone with some of the most accessible entry requirements in the Americas. Its cities pulse with culture and cuisine; safety varies widely by sub-zone.

quests.label: Temporary or Permanent Resident Visa
quests.detail: FM3 Temporary Resident allows work with employer; Residente Permanente after 4 years.

quests.label: Proof of Income or Savings
quests.detail: ~$1,600 USD/month income or ~$25,000 USD savings for temporary residency.

zones.name: Moldova
zones.lore: Moldova is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

quests.detail: Employer documentation and local registration required.

zones.name: Myanmar

zones.name: Namibia
zones.lore: Sparse-population Southern African state with mining, logistics, and tourism value.

quests.detail: Employment-based permits require sponsor and proof of role necessity.

zones.name: Nepal
zones.lore: Himalayan nation with tourism and services opportunities and low baseline cost profile.

quests.label: Work visa and permit process
quests.detail: Employment pathways depend on sponsoring entities and permit filings.

zones.name: Netherlands
zones.lore: Flat, orderly, and extremely international. The 30% tax ruling offers a legendary financial buff to skilled migrants, though Amsterdam housing has become a brutal resource war.

quests.label: Highly Skilled Migrant or EU Blue Card
quests.detail: Highly Skilled Migrant requires salary ≥ €4,171/month (2025).

zones.name: New Caledonia
zones.lore: New Caledonia is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: New Zealand
zones.lore: Stunningly beautiful and fiercely protective of its ecological balance. Immigration quotas make entry competitive, but those who arrive find one of the most balanced zones in the game.

quests.label: Skilled Migrant or AEWV
quests.detail: Skilled Migrant Category (points-based) or Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV).

zones.name: Nicaragua
zones.lore: Low-cost Central American route with agriculture, trade, and small services sectors.

quests.label: Temporary residence and work authorization
quests.detail: Long-stay routes generally require local sponsor/business documentation.

zones.name: Niger
zones.lore: Sahelian state with severe security and infrastructure challenges across many regions.

quests.label: High-risk mobility and permit constraints
quests.detail: Entry and internal movement can be heavily restricted.

zones.name: Nigeria
zones.lore: Africa’s largest population center with major fintech growth and large market demand.

zones.name: Oman
zones.lore: Stable Gulf state with measured diversification and strong logistics/shipping position.

quests.label: Employer sponsorship and residence card
quests.detail: Most work routes require sponsor-backed labor clearances.

zones.name: Pakistan
zones.lore: Large and youthful economy with major textile, agriculture, and services clusters.

quests.label: Employment visa + registration
quests.detail: Company sponsorship and permit compliance needed for long-term work.

zones.lore: Dollarized logistics hub centered on canal trade and regional finance/services.

zones.lore: Papua New Guinea is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Paraguay
zones.lore: Low-tax, low-cost inland economy with straightforward residence pathways.

quests.label: Temporary/permanent residence process
quests.detail: Common pathways include economic activity and local compliance docs.

zones.name: China
zones.lore: Manufacturing superpower with advanced urban clusters and structured permit systems.

quests.label: Z visa + work permit
quests.detail: Employer sponsorship and permit class rating required.

zones.name: Peru
zones.lore: Rapidly growing Pacific-Andean economy with strong mining, services, and tourism potential.

quests.label: Worker or professional residency
quests.detail: Contract-based and professional pathways with registration steps.

zones.name: Philippines
zones.lore: Archipelago economy with major BPO, maritime, and services sectors and strong English usage.

quests.label: Work visa + alien permit
quests.detail: Employment requires visa sponsorship and labor permit filings.

zones.name: Poland
zones.lore: A rapidly ascending zone that has become the tech hub of Eastern Europe. Relatively affordable with EU access — a compelling value proposition for skilled workers.

quests.label: Polish Work Visa (Type D)
quests.detail: National visa for work; employer must file a declaration of intent to employ.

zones.name: Portugal
zones.lore: Once a sleepy coastal zone, now one of the most contested destinations for digital nomads. Its golden shores offer EU residency pathways that larger zones guard with far heavier locks.

quests.label: Portugal Visa or EU Residency
quests.detail: D7 Passive Income Visa, Digital Nomad Visa (D8), or Golden Visa.

quests.label: Proof of Income / Remote Work
quests.detail: D7 requires ~€760/month; Digital Nomad requires €3,040/month.

zones.name: Puerto Rico
zones.lore: Puerto Rico is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Qatar
zones.lore: High-income Gulf microstate with strong energy revenue and concentrated urban economy.

quests.label: Sponsor-backed employment status
quests.detail: Work and stay are typically tied to employer sponsorship.

zones.name: Republic of the Congo
zones.lore: Central African oil-linked economy with concentrated urban services.

quests.label: Work authorization and residence card
quests.detail: Sponsor-backed permits are common for foreign employment.

zones.name: Romania
zones.lore: Rapidly modernizing EU member with competitive tech talent and lower operating costs.

quests.label: Work authorization and residence registration
quests.detail: Employer sponsorship remains typical for non-EU nationals.

zones.name: Russia
zones.lore: Russia is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Rwanda
zones.lore: Policy-stable East African growth state with emphasis on digital services and governance.

quests.label: Work visa and residency
quests.detail: Employer-supported permits and local registration are typical.

zones.name: Saudi Arabia
zones.lore: High-income Gulf economy with large-scale infrastructure and diversification programs.

quests.label: Employer sponsorship + iqama
quests.detail: Most foreign workers require sponsor-backed residence/work status.

zones.name: Senegal
zones.lore: Stable Atlantic gateway with growing digital services and regional trade relevance.

quests.label: Professional visa and registration
quests.detail: Employer-backed documentation generally required.

zones.name: Serbia
zones.lore: Serbia is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Sierra Leone
zones.lore: Sierra Leone is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Singapore
zones.lore: The most efficient city-state ever built. Clean, safe, and extraordinarily wealthy — but permanent residence is a legendary endgame quest that most adventurers never complete.

quests.label: Employment Pass or S Pass
quests.detail: Employment Pass for professionals earning S$5,000+/month; S Pass for mid-skill.

quests.label: Minimum Salary S$5,000/month
quests.detail: Employment Pass requires S$5,000/month minimum (S$5,500 for financial services).

zones.name: Slovakia
zones.lore: Automotive and manufacturing-heavy EU economy with lower costs than Western core markets.

quests.label: Work permit + temporary residence
quests.detail: Employer process plus police registration for residence.

zones.name: Slovenia
zones.lore: Small high-quality EU economy with strong logistics and manufacturing connectivity.

quests.label: Single permit process
quests.detail: Employment contract and permit filing required for most non-EU workers.

zones.name: Solomon Islands
zones.lore: Solomon Islands is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Somalia
zones.lore: Somalia is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: South Africa
zones.lore: Stunning landscapes and extraordinary biodiversity. Extreme inequality is its defining tension — world-class and deeply challenged exist side by side at every level.

quests.label: Critical Skills or General Work Visa
quests.detail: Critical Skills visa for shortage occupations. General Work Visa requires employer offer.

zones.name: South Korea
zones.lore: Hyper-connected and relentlessly innovative. Its digital infrastructure is the envy of the world. Cultural integration is the game's hardest hidden quest.

quests.label: Korea Work Visa (E-Series)
quests.detail: E-7 Specially Designated Activities, E-2 Language Instruction, or F-2 Residency.

zones.name: South Sudan
zones.lore: South Sudan is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Spain
zones.lore: Land of eternal sunshine and late dinners. Spain's Digital Nomad Visa and Non-Lucrative Visa attract thousands annually, though bureaucratic quest chains are notoriously long.

quests.label: Spain NLV, DNV, or Work Visa
quests.detail: Non-Lucrative Visa (passive income), Digital Nomad Visa, or work authorization.

zones.name: Sri Lanka
zones.lore: Island economy with tourism, logistics, and services opportunities in a compact geography.

quests.label: Residence/work visa categories
quests.detail: Business and employment pathways require documentation and approvals.

zones.name: Sudan
zones.lore: High-volatility environment with significant operational and security uncertainty.

quests.label: High-risk entry environment
quests.detail: Movement, work, and long-stay permissions can be unstable and restrictive.

zones.name: Suriname
zones.lore: Suriname is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Sweden
zones.lore: Land of midnight sun and exceptional work-life balance. Sweden's social systems are the envy of the world — funded by high taxes that require substantial income to absorb comfortably.

quests.label: Swedish Work Permit
quests.detail: Must have a job offer; employer advertises role in EU for 10 days first.

zones.name: Switzerland
zones.lore: Perched in the Alps with the highest wages and cost of living in Europe. Switzerland guards its borders with quiet precision — annual quotas make entry feel almost mythical.

quests.label: Swiss Work Permit (L or B)
quests.detail: Non-EU nationals require employer sponsorship and are subject to annual quotas.

quests.label: Annual Immigration Quota
quests.detail: Switzerland controls immigration via strict annual quotas for non-EU/EFTA nationals.

zones.name: Syria
zones.lore: Syria is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Taiwan
zones.lore: Taiwan is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Tajikistan
zones.lore: Tajikistan is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Tanzania
zones.lore: Large East African market with growing urban corridors and major logistics potential.

quests.label: Work permit + residence permit
quests.detail: Employer support and permit sequencing required.

zones.name: Thailand
zones.lore: A beloved starter zone for digital nomads worldwide. The cost of living is legendary in its leniency, and the food buffs are some of the most powerful known to travelers.

quests.label: Thai LTR or Non-Immigrant Visa
quests.detail: Long-Term Resident (LTR) visa for high-earners; tourist visa for short stays.

zones.name: Bahamas
zones.lore: Tourism and offshore-finance-driven island economy with high import-linked costs.

quests.label: Employer-led work permit
quests.detail: Long-stay work requires sponsorship and permit approvals.

zones.name: The Gambia
zones.lore: The Gambia is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Togo
zones.lore: Togo is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Trinidad and Tobago
zones.lore: Energy-rich Caribbean economy with concentrated urban-industrial activity.

quests.label: Work permit route
quests.detail: Employer sponsorship and permit approvals required for non-citizen employment.

zones.name: Tunisia
zones.lore: Compact North African economy with export manufacturing and services potential.

quests.label: Work authorization and residence permit
quests.detail: Employer-backed status remains the standard route.

zones.name: Turkey
zones.lore: Bridge economy between Europe and Asia with major logistics, industry, and services hubs.

quests.label: Residence permit (ikamet) pathway
quests.detail: Work and residence processes depend on sponsorship and permit category.

zones.name: Turkmenistan
zones.lore: Turkmenistan is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Uganda
zones.lore: Fast-growing inland market with strong agricultural base and rising urban demand.

quests.label: Entry/work permit process
quests.detail: Work permits require sponsor and immigration approvals.

zones.name: Ukraine
zones.lore: A zone of extraordinary cultural depth, currently in active conflict. Entry for non-essential purposes is not advisable. Its people demonstrate remarkable resilience under siege.

quests.label: ⚠️ Active Conflict Zone
quests.detail: Most governments advise against all travel. Entry requires exceptional justification.

quests.label: Ukraine Temporary Stay
quests.detail: Visa-free for many nationalities (90 days), but entry is currently extremely inadvisable.

zones.name: United Arab Emirates
zones.lore: A tax-free desert citadel that emerged from the sand in a single generation. Zero income tax is the most powerful buff in the zone, but political freedoms are limited.

quests.label: UAE Residence or Golden Visa
quests.detail: Employment visa sponsored by employer, or Golden Visa (10 years) for investors/skilled.

zones.name: United Kingdom
zones.lore: Once a gateway to the European continent, Brexit sealed its walls and rebuilt them with a points-based system. London remains one of the most competitive battlegrounds in the world.

quests.label: UK Skilled Worker Visa
quests.detail: Points-based: 70 points from salary, skill level, English, sponsorship.

quests.label: Minimum Salary £38,700/year
quests.detail: 2025 minimum salary threshold for Skilled Worker Visa.

zones.name: United States
zones.lore: The most contested zone on the map. Immense wealth and opportunity lie within, but its gates are guarded by labyrinthine bureaucracy and decade-long quest chains.

quests.label: US Visa or Green Card
quests.detail: Work visa (H-1B, L-1, O-1) or permanent residency required to work legally.

quests.label: Employer Sponsorship
quests.detail: Most work visas require a US employer to sponsor your application.

quests.label: Background & Medical Check
quests.detail: Criminal background check and medical examination required.

zones.name: Uruguay
zones.lore: Stable, high-governance South American option with comparatively strong institutions.

quests.label: Legal residence and cédula process
quests.detail: Economic proof and local registration support long-term residence.

zones.name: Uzbekistan
zones.lore: Fast-reforming Central Asian economy with expanding services and manufacturing sectors.

quests.label: Work visa and registration
quests.detail: Employer support and local registration are required for long stays.

zones.name: Vanuatu
zones.lore: Vanuatu is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Venezuela
zones.lore: Resource-rich nation facing severe economic and governance instability in recent years.

quests.label: High-risk operating environment
quests.detail: Entry, safety, and legal processes can be volatile and highly context-dependent.

zones.name: Vietnam
zones.lore: Fast-growing manufacturing and tech destination with strong urban growth corridors.

quests.label: Work permit + temporary residence
quests.detail: Permit exemptions exist, but most long-term roles require employer-backed permits.

zones.name: Western Sahara
zones.lore: Western Sahara is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Yemen
zones.lore: Yemen is now mapped as a baseline world zone. Detailed regional content can be layered in later phases.

quests.label: Entry and residency pathway
quests.detail: Check official immigration channels for visa, permit, and residency requirements.

zones.name: Zambia
zones.lore: Resource-linked economy with expanding mining, energy, and agribusiness sectors.

quests.label: Employment permit route
quests.detail: Permit process requires sponsor and role-specific approvals.

zones.name: Zimbabwe
zones.lore: Agriculture and mining-centered economy with periodic policy and currency instability.

quests.label: Work permit and residence process
quests.detail: Employer-backed route with regular compliance updates.

zones.name: Bahrain
zones.lore: Compact Gulf financial center with diversified services and regional banking role.

quests.label: Employer-sponsored work visa
quests.detail: Residence/work status is generally tied to sponsoring employer.
